key_insight: Large-scale machine-learned simulations reveal that amorphous indium oxide contains quasi-crystalline edge-sharing polyhedral chains and O-O peroxide-like bonds that explain its high mobility and intrinsic n-type behavior.
engineering_relevance: These structural insights can guide the design of amorphous In2O3 thin films and photoanodes with improved electron transport and optoelectronic performance.
method: MACE machine-learned interatomic potential trained on first-principles molecular dynamics
Large-scale amorphous structure generation and analysis up to 5120 atoms
Comparison of computed X-ray structure factors, Raman signatures, and electronic structure with experiment
evidence_signal: The simulations achieve excellent quantitative agreement with experimental X-ray structure factors and predict a distinct Raman feature near 850 cm^-1 linked to peroxide-like bonds.
watch_item: The conclusions rely on a trained interatomic potential, so transferability to other compositions, defect levels, or processing conditions should be validated carefully.
paper_title: Large-scale first-principle simulations of amorphous indium oxide
